title: Can France's right and center agree on a common presidential candidate?
subhed: While some supporters of President Emmanuel Macron have called for a joint primary with the right-wing Les Républicains, the main candidates have refused to take part, raising questions about whether their platforms are even compatible.
byline: By Mariama Darame and Marie Pouzadoux

Macron adviser appointed culture minister in mini-reshuffle
subhed: Catherine Pégard, 71, was the head of the Château de Versailles for more than 12 years. She will have to oversee several major projects, including the Louvre renovation project.

title: Anthropoic says it will not give US government unrestricted use of AI
subhed: Despite pressure from the Pentagon, the AI company said it would not give the US Defense Department unconditional use of its technology. The US government has threatened forced compliance under federal emergency powers.

title: 'The Epstein scandal is astonishing by the scale of the complicity and rape culture at the top of society'
subhed: The philosopher Marc Crépon, who specializes in the forms and effects of violence, analyzes the public response to the sex scandal in an interview with Le Monde. He warns against the risk of disregarding the suffering and trauma of the victims.

title: How Paris is turning into 'Frollywood,' a refuge for American actors
subhed: Whether George Clooney, Matt Dillon or Kristen Stewart, France is attracting more and more actors from across the Atlantic. This trend speaks to both the Trump-era crisis and the decline of the Hollywood model.
byline: By Laurent Carpentier and Aureliano Tonet

The Langs' troubling friendship with Jeffrey Epstein
subhed: Former French culture minister Jack Lang and his daughter Caroline Lang appear thousands of times in the most recently released documents. All evidence suggests that she had a much closer relationship with the sex offender than she now admits.

title: 'Trump is the president of instability and uncertainty, increasingly to his own detriment'
subhed: By continually flouting the rules, railing against institutions and single-handedly steering his country toward a new war, the president of the United States has demonstrated nothing but his inability to act as a statesman, observes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

title: Four years of war in Ukraine: Five photographers on assignment for Le Monde share their most striking images from 2025
subhed: Five photographers who covered the conflict in 2025 have each chosen an emblematic image of the conflict. Beyond the official figures and statements, these unique perspectives reveal the mark that this new year of war has left on them.
